intitule: Word Formation: Noun Formation from Verbs and Adjectives

enonce.texte:
Lesson 26: Word Formation (Noun Formation from Verbs/Adjectives)

Rule Reminder: Many nouns are formed by adding suffixes to verbs or adjectives. Common suffixes: -tion, -sion, -ment, -ance, -ence, -ity, -ness, -y.

Examples:

  • educate (verb) → education (noun)
  • beautiful (adjective) → beauty (noun)
  • develop (verb) → development (noun)
  • important (adjective) → importance (noun)

Complete the table below by forming the correct noun from each verb or adjective.

questions.texte: Complete the table with the correct noun form.

intitule: Word Formation: Prefixes and Suffixes (Opposites and Word Changes)

enonce.texte:
Lesson 26: Prefixes and Suffixes

Rule Reminder: Prefixes are added to the beginning of a word to change its meaning (often to the opposite). Common negative prefixes: un-, in-, im-, il-, ir-, dis-, non-.

Examples:

  • ripe → unripe (not ripe)
  • legal → illegal (not legal)
  • agree → disagree (opposite of agree)

Add the correct prefix to each word to form its opposite.

questions.texte: Add the correct negative prefix to each word.

enonce.texte:
Lesson 26: Antonyms and Synonyms

Rule Reminder: Synonyms are words with similar meanings (e.g., happy - joyful). Antonyms are words with opposite meanings (e.g., hot - cold).

Match each word with its synonym (Column A) and its antonym (Column B).

questions.texte: Match each word with the correct synonym and antonym.

intitule: Real-Life Problem Solving: Using Word Formation in Context

enonce.texte:
Lesson 26: Word Formation in Real Life

Read the short passage about a student named Amina. Then complete the tasks using your knowledge of word formation (nouns, prefixes, suffixes, antonyms, synonyms).

Amina is a hardworking student in Yaoundé. She wants to become a doctor to help people in her community. However, her school lacks proper science equipment. This makes her (1) ________ (patient) sometimes, but she never gives up. Her teachers (2) ________ (courage) her to study hard. Amina believes that (3) ________ (educate) is the key to success. She also thinks that (4) ________ (pollute) is a serious problem in her city, and she wants to find solutions.

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the correct word form using the clues in parentheses.

sousQuestions.texte: Blank (1): ________ (patient - use negative prefix + noun form)
Blank (2): ________ (courage - add suffix to make a verb)

questions.texte: Find a synonym for 'hardworking' in the passage.
questions.reponse: There is no direct synonym in the passage, but possible synonyms include: diligent, industrious, dedicated.

questions.texte: Form the opposite of 'serious' using a prefix.
Write one sentence of advice for Amina using the word 'development' (noun form of 'develop').
questions.reponse: Example: 'Amina, the development of your skills will help you become a great doctor.'

intitule: Real-Life Writing Task: Word Formation in a Formal Letter

enonce.texte:
Lesson 26: Applying Word Formation in Writing

Imagine you are a student representative in your school in Cameroon. Your school library lacks books on science and technology. Write a short formal letter (80-100 words) to the school principal requesting new books.

Requirements: Use at least:

  • 2 nouns formed from verbs (e.g., education, development, improvement)
  • 1 word with a negative prefix (e.g., impossible, inadequate, unfinished)
  • 1 synonym for 'important'
  • 1 antonym for 'old'

questions.texte: Write your formal letter in the space below.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

Dear Principal,

I am writing to request an improvement (noun from improve) of our school library. The lack of science books creates an impossible (negative prefix) situation for students preparing for exams. Access to modern (antonym of old) books is essential (synonym for important) for our education (noun from educate). Your support will make a great difference (noun from differ) to our learning. Thank you.

Yours sincerely,
Student Representative
